Metadata.title: AnswerLint — linting your website for AI answers
Metadata.description: AnswerLint scans websites for answer readiness, citation trust, and AI search release risk. Audit pages for ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, and AI Overviews.

Hero.eyebrow: Linting your website for AI answers
Hero.title: Lint your website before AI ignores it.
Hero.subtitle: AnswerLint audits whether ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, and AI Overviews can understand, trust, and cite your pages.
Hero.questionsLead: Know which launch pages answer the buyer question, which signals are missing, and where competitors are easier for AI systems to quote.
Hero.businessLead: Start with the business map, then inspect individual pages when you need deeper evidence.
Hero.panelTitle: One audit surface for answers and citations
Hero.panelBusinessBody: This page is strong at direct answers, but trust and freshness signals are leaving citation share on the table. Fixing schema and source attribution should improve visibility fastest.
Hero.panelDeveloperFix1: Add FAQPage or HowTo schema for answer extraction.
Hero.panelDeveloperFix2: Publish a visible author and `dateModified` signal.
Hero.panelDeveloperFix3: Increase authoritative external citations in core sections.

Why.title: Why teams need a stricter visibility layer
Why.lead: AI discovery does not reward pages just because they exist. It rewards pages that answer cleanly, cite clearly, and expose enough trust for a model to use them.
Why.p1: AEO helps you become the answer. GEO helps AI systems treat your page as a reliable source. AnswerLint audits both so the team can see the whole risk profile.
Why.p2: Think ESLint for AI-ready content: one repeatable score, one evidence trail, and one release bar for content, SEO, and engineering.
Why.card1Body: A page can rank and still miss AI answers. Weak structure and weak sources cause that.
Why.card2Body: Leaders want risk and impact. Devs want clear markup fixes. Many audits speak to only one group.
Why.card3Body: Compare side by side. Then you know if a low score is OK—or if a rival wins AI cites.
Why.quote: If answer visibility matters, it needs a release standard.

Audience.title: One story for revenue owners and build teams.
Audience.subtitle: Say why this helps the business. Say how fast the team can act.
Audience.businessBody: Lead with reach, trust, and rival risk. Say what the score means. Say how it hits demand.
Audience.businessBullet1: Explain whether the page is likely to be quoted or skipped.
Audience.businessBullet2: Call out the commercial risk of weak trust and freshness signals.
Audience.businessBullet3: Summarize the top 3 changes that most improve discoverability.
Audience.developerBody: Next, list the exact fixes. Tech buyers stay hooked. Other teams still get value.
Audience.developerBullet1: Map every score to a concrete check and evidence.
Audience.developerBullet2: Show schema, heading, citation, and metadata gaps clearly.
Audience.developerBullet3: Keep JSON, CSV, and CI outputs ready for pipelines.

Education.title: AEO & GEO in plain language
Education.subtitle: Both aim for visibility when answers are generated, not just ten blue links. AnswerLint checks your content with that lens.
Education.aeoBody: AEO is for engines that pull short answers. Think snippets, voice, and Q&A blocks. Use clear headings and facts. That helps you get cited.
Education.aeoProduct: AnswerLint shows gaps in clarity and structure. It shows what models need to quote you.
Education.geoBody: GEO is for AI that reads many pages at once. Use one brand name. Add facts you can trace. Use clean HTML. That cuts wrong AI facts and keeps your brand right.
Education.geoProduct: AnswerLint scores GEO with AEO. You see combined risk, not one vanity score.

Explainer.title: Show the market how visibility moves from ranking to being cited.
Explainer.subtitle: This chart sits after the hero. It explains the idea fast. It helps founders and ops trust the story.
Explainer.imageAlt: Explainer graphic showing how SEO connects to AEO and GEO in AI search

How.title: How AnswerLint works
How.subtitle: From a URL or file to a report you can share in CI or a browser.
How.step1Body: Point the CLI at a URL, a Markdown or HTML file, a folder, or a sitemap. It honors rate limits and robots.txt unless you override on your own site.
How.step2Body: Checks run on your content. Failed checks explain why. Writers and devs see what to fix.
How.step3Body: You get combined, AEO, and GEO scores. Add .answerlint.json for project defaults if you want.
How.step4Body: Print HTML for people, JSON for scripts, or CSV for batches. Use --ci and --threshold to fail builds when scores drop.

Workflow.title: How do teams use AnswerLint with GitHub, Vercel, and HTTPS pages?
Workflow.subtitle: These are the practical workflows teams care about when they move from one-off checks to repeatable release habits.
Workflow.card1Title: How do developers audit HTTPS pages before release?
Workflow.card1Body: Teams run AnswerLint against production-like HTTPS URLs so they can review answer readiness, trust signals, and citation gaps before a page ships.
Workflow.card2Title: How do teams use AnswerLint with GitHub and CI?
Workflow.card2Body: Teams export HTML and JSON reports into GitHub workflows, compare score changes across releases, and block merges when the agreed bar drops.
Workflow.card3Title: What type of report fits Vercel previews and production sites?
Workflow.card3Body: HTML works for human review, JSON fits automation, and CSV helps content ops benchmark many URLs or compare a launch page against a competitor.

Comparison.title: AnswerLint vs manual review vs generic AI prompts
Comparison.subtitle: Should you audit by hand? Use a chat model? Or run an answer-readiness linter on each release? This lines up three paths.
Comparison.row1AnswerLint: AEO, GEO, and combined scores you can export.
Comparison.row1Manual: Often subjective. Scores drift by reviewer.
Comparison.row1Generic: It can sum things up. It does not give a steady audit line.
Comparison.row2AnswerLint: Runs local, in CI, and in batch reviews.
Comparison.row2Manual: Hard to scale across many pages or ships.
Comparison.row2Generic: Fine for ideas. Weak for rules and release gates.
Comparison.row3AnswerLint: Returns HTML, JSON, and CSV with clear findings.
Comparison.row3Manual: Proof often lives in notes or sheets.
Comparison.row3Generic: Quality depends on the prompt. It is hard to compare runs.
Comparison.row4AnswerLint: Teams that need one list of what to fix next.
Comparison.row4Manual: Small one-off reviews or editorial spot checks.

Product.title: An enterprise-grade audit loop inside an open-source CLI.
Product.subtitle: AnswerLint is designed for teams that want AI visibility measurement to live beside their normal shipping workflow.
Product.card1Body: HTML for stakeholders, JSON for pipelines, CSV for sitemap or folder runs.
Product.card2Body: Optional .answerlint.json in the repo or home directory; override path per run.
Product.card3Body: Exit codes for pass, score failure, network errors, and bad input—mirror how you run Lighthouse in CI.
Product.card4Body: Read-only fetches, configurable rate limits, and robots awareness for compliant crawling.
Product.card5Body: AnswerLint overview surfaces capabilities, outputs, and quick-start hints in the terminal.
Product.card6Body: Compare one live URL against a competitor URL to see score deltas, parity gaps, and first fixes.

Action.title: Run the same inspection locally, in preview, and in CI.
Action.subtitle: Start with the playground, then move the workflow into the repo so every important page ships with an answer-visibility bar.
Action.imageAlt: Terminal screenshot of the AnswerLint capability dashboard
Action.detail1: Run the same checks locally, in CI, or across a sitemap.
Action.detail2: Share a readable report with non-technical stakeholders without hiding the underlying evidence.
Action.detail3: Use the playground for fast exploration, then move the workflow into your repo.

Report.title: A report that executives can read and builders can execute.
Report.subtitle: The strongest audit experience explains commercial risk first, then backs it with exact evidence and implementation details.
Report.businessTitle: What business users should see first
Report.business1: A short summary of whether this page is likely to earn trust and citations.
Report.business2: A simple statement of competitive position: behind, equal, or ahead.
Report.business3: Three highest-value actions tied to visibility impact, not technical jargon.
Report.developerTitle: What developers should see immediately after
Report.developer1: Audit evidence for each failed check, including the exact missing signal.
Report.developer2: Suggested schema, content structure, citation, and metadata improvements.
Report.developer3: Machine-readable outputs for CI gates, diffing, and automation.
Report.sampleTitle: Readable summary with technical depth underneath
Report.summaryBody: Answer readiness is promising, but the page is under-signaling trust. Improve freshness, citation quality, and structured guidance to make it more quotable.
Report.fix1: Publish stronger authorship and freshness metadata.
Report.fix2: Expand support links to trusted external sources.
Report.fix3: Turn core sections into answer-first blocks and FAQ markup.

Compatibility.title: Compatibility
Compatibility.subtitle: Runs anywhere you run Node—local, CI, or container.

Faq.title: What do teams usually ask before they adopt AnswerLint?
Faq.subtitle: Content, SEO, and devs ask these when they pick AI visibility tools.
Faq.q1: What does AnswerLint help content teams measure?
Faq.a1: It checks if a page can be the answer and the source. One report scores clarity, structure, trust, dates, and cite fit.
Faq.q2: How does AnswerLint compare with manual reviews?
Faq.a2: Manual review can spark ideas. It is slow and uneven. AnswerLint gives a steady audit, a score you can track, and fixes for writers, SEO, and devs.
Faq.q3: Can developers use AnswerLint in CI and automation?
Faq.a3: Yes. Devs can run it on URLs, files, folders, and sitemaps. They can export HTML, JSON, or CSV. They can set CI floors to block ships when scores slip.
Faq.q4: Why do AEO and GEO both matter for B2B teams?
Faq.a4: AEO helps when buyers ask plain questions. GEO helps when AI picks which sites to trust. B2B teams need both to show up in AI-led discovery.

Reviews.title: What people say
Reviews.subtitle: Real quotes from users will appear here as they come in.
Reviews.emptyBody: We are collecting feedback from teams using AnswerLint. Check back, or star the repo to follow releases.

Cta.title: Give every launch page a sharper AI answer-readiness review.
Cta.subtitle: Use the playground for quick exploration, then move the same workflow into the CLI and CI. Reports stay local and under your control.

Footer.rights: AnswerLint is MIT licensed. Respect robots.txt, terms of use, and rate limits when auditing third-party sites.
